verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Soon after, Constantius seems to have asked leave of St. Chrysostom to join him; for in his 13th letter to Olympias on arriving at Cocussus or Cucusus in Cappadocia, now Goksyn, his bitter place of exile, St. Chrysostom says (ib. p.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 594), “My Lord, the most pious priest Constantius, would fain have been here long ago, for he wrote to me begging that I would let him come.”

verses.text: To Constantius’ piety we owe the preservation of these Homilies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: One very special value of them lies in the pious fervent exhortation at the end of each, on Penitence, Almsgiving, or whatever St. Chrysostom had at the time chiefly in mind, breathing forth words from a heart, filled with the love of God and that longed for his flock to partake it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: [It has seemed better in this edition to conform the translation of the Scripture texts to some one standard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: St. Chrysostom used the current text of his day, which, on the whole, was more like the Textus Receptus, the basis of the A.V., than the more critical text followed by the R.V.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It has therefore seemed best to take the A.V. as the standard (except where St. Chrysostom has followed a different text), but note has been made of any variations of the R.V. materially affecting the sense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There remain a number of loose quotations and combinations of different texts, and in these the English translation is retained.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Effort has been made to simplify the language and remove involved constructions in the translation of the Homilies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The English translation was originally made from the Benedictine, and afterwards revised from Field’s more accurate text, and the differences between these have sometimes been overlooked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Besides this, it has often been possible to give St. Chrysostom’s meaning more accurately,—sometimes even reversing the sense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There are, however, many very felicitous translations in the English edition which have been retained.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is a revision, and not a new translation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: All the notes in the English edition have been scrupulously retained, additions being enclosed in square brackets, with the initials of the reviser.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: An introduction on the authorship of this Epistle has been inserted.—F.G.]
